mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing present values of future corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 205 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 205.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising aggregate wages, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures where consumption outpaces GDP signals robust consumer-driven demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consumption outpacing GDP yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines.
